mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth acceleration (GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter expansion rate) directly signals strengthening corporate earnings capacity. Positive GDP surprises relative to trend produce positive S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days as equity valuations expand on improved growth expectations.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth quarters do not produce statistically significantly higher forward returns than negative growth quarters (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases corporate borrowing costs and reduces equity risk appetite. Elevated FEDFUNDS produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market participants price in reduced leverage capacity and lower net present value of future cash flows.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 5-day forward returns is positive and exceeds 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. A 0.3 percentage point increase in UNRATE over 3 months produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity markets discount lower corporate revenues from weakened consumer demand.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, S&P 500 produces negative 20-day forward returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE change and 20-day forward returns is negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand. When both series decline simultaneously, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward and anticipates earnings compression across consumer-sensitive sectors.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint decline) does not produce significantly more negative returns than either series declining alone (interaction t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 2% year-over-year signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power. Elevated INDPRO produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity markets reward improved operational efficiency and margin expansion in manufacturing-heavy index constituents.
failure_condition: When INDPRO year-over-year growth exceeds 2%, S&P 500 produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 20-day forward returns is negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) deceleration below 100,000 net new jobs per month combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3.5% year-over-year) signals stagflationary pressure. This interaction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as real earnings compress and equity risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100k AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year > 3.5%
failure_condition: When both conditions are met (weak payroll growth and elevated inflation), S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect does not produce significantly more negative returns than either condition alone (interaction t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate over 3 months signals monetary policy easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases leverage capacity for corporations. Rate cuts produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market participants immediately reprice equity valuations upward.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS declines > 0.25pp in 3 months, S&P 500 produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS change and 5-day forward returns is negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1,400 thousand units annualized signals robust residential investment demand and forward-looking consumer confidence. Elevated housing construction produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market prices in sustained aggregate demand and multiplier effects across construction, materials, and consumer discretionary sectors.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1,400 thousand units, S&P 500 produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between HOUST level and 60-day forward returns is negative over any 2-year rolling window.
